title: astragalus chamaephaca
intro: Astragalus chamaephaca, also known as the Tufted Milk Vetch, is a perennial herb belonging to the legume family and is native to mountainous regions of the western United States. The plant's roots and flowers are used in herbal medicine, showcasing its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory activities. The health benefits of Astragalus chamaephaca are attributed to its ability to enhance the body's immune system, reduce stress and fatigue, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial properties. Astragalus chamaephaca is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, with the root being the most commonly used part for herbal preparations.
